growth_records.strain: P. dorei 5_1_36 (BEI HM-29) + L. symbiosum WAL-14673 (BEI HM-319)

up.method: TLC + phenol-sulfuric acid fructose quantification; near-total inulin consumption by ~24 h in co-culture

sec.method: HPLC of bioreactor supernatant at 26 h; acetate reached higher concentration in inulin co-culture (numeric value only in Fig. S1/S2, not in text)
HPLC of bioreactor supernatant at 26 h; butyrate reached higher concentration in inulin co-culture (numeric value only in figure)
HPLC of bioreactor supernatant at 26 h; lactate concentration similar in both fibers (numeric value only in figure)

growth_records.cite: Hirmas et al. Metabolic Modeling and Bidirectional Culturing of Two Gut Microbes Reveal Cross-Feeding Interactions and Protective Effects on Intestinal Cells. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00646-22. [PMC9600892]
growth_records.snippet: Experimentally observed community growth rates at midexponential phase growing on inulin and xylan were 0.35 h-1 and 0.09 h-1, respectively (12 h <= t <= 24 h). ... microbial abundances were stable and were 0.159 on inulin ... for P. dorei,
growth_records.notes: Community (co-culture) specific growth rate mu = 0.35 h-1 measured experimentally from OD600 slope over 12-24 h midexponential phase in bioreactor (Fig. S1). Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.35 = 1.98 h (calculated). Relative abundances at steady state: P. dorei 0.159, L. symbiosum 0.841 (experimental). Propionate and succinate NOT detected in bioreactor supernatants. SCFA/lactate concentrations reported only graphically (Fig. S1/S2), so numeric secretion values are not extractable from text. Base mZMB is a complex medium (from ref 48/83); components not itemized, so composition_stated=false. Model exchange fluxes (Fig. 2, mmol/h) are SteadyCom-PREDICTED not experimental, hence not entered as mfa_fluxes.

growth_records.strain: P. dorei 5_1_36 (BEI HM-29) + L. symbiosum WAL-14673 (BEI HM-319)

up.method: TLC + xylose quantification; only P. dorei degrades xylan; co-culture shows reduced total carbohydrate at 48 h

sec.method: HPLC of bioreactor supernatant at 48 h (numeric value only in figure)
HPLC of bioreactor supernatant at 48 h; lower than inulin run (numeric value only in figure)
HPLC of bioreactor supernatant at 48 h (numeric value only in figure)

growth_records.cite: Hirmas et al. Metabolic Modeling and Bidirectional Culturing of Two Gut Microbes Reveal Cross-Feeding Interactions and Protective Effects on Intestinal Cells. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00646-22. [PMC9600892]
growth_records.snippet: Experimentally observed community growth rates ... on inulin and xylan were 0.35 h-1 and 0.09 h-1 ... Co-cultures in xylan only reached a maximum optical density (OD) of 1.5 ... microbial abundances ... 0.091 on xylan for P. dorei, and ...
growth_records.notes: Community (co-culture) specific growth rate mu = 0.09 h-1 measured from OD600 slope over 12-24 h midexponential phase in bioreactor. Doubling time = ln2/0.09 = 0.6931/0.09 = 7.70 h (calculated). Max OD600 ~1.5; xylan fermentation slower, stationary reached later (run 48 h). Relative abundances: P. dorei 0.091, L. symbiosum 0.909 (experimental). Propionate and succinate NOT detected in bioreactor supernatants. SCFA concentrations only in figure. Model fluxes are predicted (SteadyCom), not experimental.

up.method: TLC + fructose (phenol-sulfuric acid); monoculture reduced total inulin after 48 h, especially intermediate and smaller fractions

sec.method: quantified in monoculture supernatant after 48 h
only P. dorei produced succinate; concentration lower on inulin than on xylan (numeric value only in figure)
propionate NOT produced on inulin (produced only on xylan / no-carbon control)

growth_records.cite: Hirmas et al. Metabolic Modeling and Bidirectional Culturing of Two Gut Microbes Reveal Cross-Feeding Interactions and Protective Effects on Intestinal Cells. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00646-22. [PMC9600892]
growth_records.snippet: Single cultures of P. dorei produced 17 mM lactate in inulin and 7 mM lactate in xylan after 48 h. ... only P. dorei produced succinate. Its concentration was higher after growing in xylan than inulin after 48 h.
growth_records.notes: P. dorei monoculture on 2% inulin (mZMB, 37C, anaerobic, 48 h): lactate 17 mM (experimental endpoint concentration, not a per-biomass rate). Succinate produced but only graphically quantified. No specific growth rate reported for monoculture; only endpoint OD600 (Fig. S2). Values are concentrations (mM), not mmol/gDW/h; qS not derivable because substrate delta and biomass (gDW) not both given numerically.

up.method: TLC + xylose quantification; only partial xylan consumption after 48 h, leaving degradation products

sec.method: quantified in monoculture supernatant after 48 h
only P. dorei produced succinate; higher on xylan than inulin (numeric value only in figure)
produced by P. dorei at low concentration on xylan (numeric value only in figure)

growth_records.cite: Hirmas et al. Metabolic Modeling and Bidirectional Culturing of Two Gut Microbes Reveal Cross-Feeding Interactions and Protective Effects on Intestinal Cells. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00646-22. [PMC9600892]
growth_records.snippet: Single cultures of P. dorei produced 17 mM lactate in inulin and 7 mM lactate in xylan after 48 h. ... P. dorei monoculture in xylan resulted only in partial substrate consumption after 48 h, leaving degradation products.
growth_records.notes: P. dorei monoculture on 2% xylan (mZMB, 37C, anaerobic, 48 h): lactate 7 mM (experimental endpoint concentration). Succinate higher than on inulin but only graphically quantified; propionate low. Only partial xylan degradation. Concentrations (mM), not per-biomass rates; qS not derivable (no gDW).
